title: 01 four attached car sheets follow moving seams
prompt: REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Image 1 locks Tina, blue hoodie, rear seat, and dark sedan. Image 2 locks the real-car / miniature-neighborhood scale and tiny lychee-helper scale. Images 3 and 4 lock flexible car-skin material and its eventual canopy continuity. INVARIANTS — Wheelbase, pillars, floor, rear seat, and Tina remain coherent and grounded. SUBSTRATE — Only the painted roof and upper door skin changes. INITIAL STATE — Blue-gray, rigid, flush with the car. APPLIED FORCE — Four teams of tiny lychee helpers pull four glowing sugar seams rearward; every helper's hands grip a seam, never a panel. TOPOLOGY — Exactly four broad continuous sheets; each begins as part of the roof/upper doors and remains attached along one lower outer edge. No sheet originates above the car. STATE CHANGE — Immediately behind each moving seam, rigid blue-gray surface becomes thin flexible pearly-pink edible membrane and rotates upward around its still-attached edge. SHOT PROOF — Wide passenger-side three-quarter at the instant all four moving boundaries are visible: blue rigid surface ahead, pink flexible sheet behind, tiny hands on the seams, Tina delighted inside. LITERALIZATION HAZARDS / VETO — no flower, petals, leaves, wings, botanical forms, freestanding canopy, convertible mechanism, torn metal, giant hand.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one polished cinematic live-action Peelerz commercial styleframe, not a collage or design sheet. Obey the countable mechanical specification before making the frame pretty. Keep one causal action readable through physical contact, tension, attached edges, and a visible active boundary. Preserve declared reference authorities. No captions, labels, watermarks, invented packaging, gore, weapons, generic gummy bears, generic magical glow, or unexplained finished transformations.
